root_text.an9.1:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.an9.1:2.2: ‘sambodhipakkhikānaṁ, āvuso, dhammānaṁ kā upanisā bhāvanāyā’ti, evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, bhikkhave, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ kinti byākareyyāthā”ti?
root_text.an9.1:2.3: “Bhagavaṁmūlakā no, bhante, dhammā …pe… bhagavato sutvā bhikkhū dhāressantī”ti.
root_text.an9.1:3.1: “Tena hi, bhikkhave, suṇātha, sādhukaṁ manasi karotha; bhāsissāmī”ti.
root_text.an9.1:3.2: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho te bhikkhū bhagavato paccassosuṁ.
root_text.an9.1:4.2: ‘sambodhipakkhikānaṁ, āvuso, dhammānaṁ kā upanisā bhāvanāyā’ti, evaṁ puṭṭhā tumhe, bhikkhave, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ evaṁ byākareyyātha:
root_text.an9.1:5.2: Sambodhipakkhikānaṁ, āvuso, dhammānaṁ ayaṁ paṭhamā upanisā bhāvanāya.
root_text.an9.1:6.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu sīlavā hoti, pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharati ācāragocarasampanno aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvī, samādāya sikkhati sikkhāpadesu.
root_text.an9.1:6.2: Sambodhipakkhikānaṁ, āvuso, dhammānaṁ ayaṁ dutiyā upanisā bhāvanāya.
root_text.an9.1:7.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu yāyaṁ kathā abhisallekhikā cetovivaraṇasappāyā, seyyathidaṁ—appicchakathā santuṭṭhikathā pavivekakathā asaṁsaggakathā vīriyārambhakathā sīlakathā samādhikathā paññākathā vimuttikathā vimuttiñāṇadassanakathā, evarūpiyā kathāya nikāmalābhī hoti akicchalābhī akasiralābhī.
root_text.an9.1:7.2: Sambodhipakkhikānaṁ, āvuso, dhammānaṁ ayaṁ tatiyā upanisā bhāvanāya.
root_text.an9.1:8.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu āraddhavīriyo viharati akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya, kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ upasampadāya, thāmavā daḷhaparakkamo anikkhittadhuro kusalesu dhammesu.
root_text.an9.1:8.2: Sambodhipakkhikānaṁ, āvuso, dhammānaṁ ayaṁ catutthī upanisā bhāvanāya.
root_text.an9.1:9.1: Puna caparaṁ, āvuso, bhikkhu paññavā hoti udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā.
root_text.an9.1:9.2: Sambodhipakkhikānaṁ, āvuso, dhammānaṁ ayaṁ pañcamī upanisā bhāvanāya’.
root_text.an9.1:10.1: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ kalyāṇasahāyassa kalyāṇasampavaṅkassa—
root_text.an9.1:10.2: sīlavā bhavissati, pātimokkhasaṁvarasaṁvuto viharissati ācāragocarasampanno aṇumattesu vajjesu bhayadassāvī, samādāya sikkhissati sikkhāpadesu.
root_text.an9.1:11.1: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ kalyāṇasahāyassa kalyāṇasampavaṅkassa—
root_text.an9.1:11.2: yāyaṁ kathā abhisallekhikā cetovivaraṇasappāyā, seyyathidaṁ—appicchakathā santuṭṭhikathā pavivekakathā asaṁsaggakathā vīriyārambhakathā sīlakathā samādhikathā paññākathā vimuttikathā vimuttiñāṇadassanakathā, evarūpiyā kathāya nikāmalābhī bhavissati akicchalābhī akasiralābhī.
root_text.an9.1:12.1: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ kalyāṇasahāyassa kalyāṇasampavaṅkassa—
root_text.an9.1:12.2: āraddhavīriyo viharissati akusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ pahānāya, kusalānaṁ dhammānaṁ upasampadāya, thāmavā daḷhaparakkamo anikkhittadhuro kusalesu dhammesu.
root_text.an9.1:13.1: Kalyāṇamittassetaṁ, bhikkhave, bhikkhuno pāṭikaṅkhaṁ kalyāṇasahāyassa kalyāṇasampavaṅkassa—
root_text.an9.1:13.2: paññavā bhavissati udayatthagāminiyā paññāya samannāgato ariyāya nibbedhikāya sammā dukkhakkhayagāminiyā.
root_text.an9.1:14.1: Tena ca pana, bhikkhave, bhikkhunā imesu pañcasu dhammesu patiṭṭhāya cattāro dhammā uttari bhāvetabbā—
root_text.an9.1:14.2: asubhā bhāvetabbā rāgassa pahānāya, mettā bhāvetabbā byāpādassa pahānāya, ānāpānassati bhāvetabbā vitakkupacchedāya, aniccasaññā bhāvetabbā asmimānasamugghātāya.
root_text.an9.1:14.4: Anattasaññī asmimānasamugghātaṁ pāpuṇāti diṭṭheva dhamme nibbānan”ti.

translation_text.an9.1:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.an9.1:1.3: There the Buddha addressed the mendicants:
translation_text.an9.1:2.1: “Mendicants, if wanderers of other religions were to ask:
translation_text.an9.1:2.2: ‘Reverends, what is the vital condition for the development of the awakening factors?’ How would you answer them?”
translation_text.an9.1:2.3: “Our teachings are rooted in the Buddha. … The mendicants will listen and remember it.”
translation_text.an9.1:3.1: “Well then, mendicants, listen and apply your mind well, I will speak.”
translation_text.an9.1:4.1: “Mendicants, if wanderers of other religions were to ask:
translation_text.an9.1:4.2: ‘Reverends, what is the vital condition for the development of the awakening factors?’ You should answer them:
translation_text.an9.1:5.1: ‘It’s when a mendicant has good friends, companions, and associates.
translation_text.an9.1:5.2: This is the first vital condition for the development of the awakening factors.
translation_text.an9.1:6.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is ethical, restrained in the monastic code, conducting themselves well and resorting for alms in suitable places. Seeing danger in the slightest fault, they keep the rules they’ve undertaken.
translation_text.an9.1:6.2: This is the second vital condition for the development of the awakening factors.
translation_text.an9.1:7.1: Furthermore, a mendicant gets to take part in talk about self-effacement that helps open the heart, when they want, without trouble or difficulty. That is, talk about fewness of wishes, contentment, seclusion, aloofness, arousing energy, ethics, immersion, wisdom, freedom, and the knowledge and vision of freedom.
translation_text.an9.1:7.2: This is the third vital condition for the development of the awakening factors.
translation_text.an9.1:8.1: Furthermore, a mendicant lives with energy roused up for giving up unskillful qualities and embracing skillful qualities. They are strong, staunchly vigorous, not slacking off when it comes to developing skillful qualities.
translation_text.an9.1:8.2: This is the fourth vital condition for the development of the awakening factors.
translation_text.an9.1:9.1: Furthermore, a mendicant is wise. They have the wisdom of arising and passing away which is noble, penetrative, and leads to the complete ending of suffering.
translation_text.an9.1:9.2: This is the fifth vital condition for the development of the awakening factors.’
translation_text.an9.1:10.1: A mendicant with good friends, companions, and associates can expect to
translation_text.an9.1:11.1: A mendicant with good friends, companions, and associates can expect to
translation_text.an9.1:11.2: take part in talk about self-effacement that helps open the heart …
translation_text.an9.1:12.1: A mendicant with good friends, companions, and associates can expect to
translation_text.an9.1:13.1: A mendicant with good friends, companions, and associates can expect to
translation_text.an9.1:14.1: But then, a mendicant grounded on these five things should develop four further things.
translation_text.an9.1:14.2: They should develop the perception of ugliness to give up greed, love to give up hate, mindfulness of breathing to cut off thinking, and perception of impermanence to uproot the conceit ‘I am’.
translation_text.an9.1:14.3: When you perceive impermanence, the perception of not-self becomes stabilized.
translation_text.an9.1:14.4: Perceiving not-self, you uproot the conceit ‘I am’ and attain extinguishment in this very life.”
